analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$76,300–$76,665 on May 1. Slight +0.24% 24h recovery but consolidating below $80K resistance. April closed +13% but momentum fading after repeated $80K rejections and $110M+ leveraged liquidations.
sentiment_key_signals: BTC ~$76,550 — repeated rejection at $80K double-top resistance
$110M+ leveraged BTC positions liquidated, downside momentum
FOMC held 3.5%–3.75% on Apr 29 — higher-for-longer signal confirmed
Fed Chair Powell term expiry May 2026 — leadership/policy uncertainty
Iran conflict + elevated oil prices — macro risk-off headwind
Crypto traders turning cautious, 47% implied probability BTC holds $76,500
Prediction market: 64% chance BTC holds above $76,000 by May 1 5pm EDT
North Korean hackers stole $6B in crypto — regulatory/security overhang
Next high-impact macro: NFP May 8, CPI May 12, PPI May 13
analysis.macro_backdrop: FOMC held rates at 3.5-3.75% on Apr 29 with higher-for-longer stance. Iran conflict keeps oil elevated, squeezing risk assets. Fed Chair transition uncertainty adds further caution. No major data release within 24h — ISM PMI today is medium-impact only. Macro filter ACTIVE (confidence 0.63 >= 0.50).
analysis.regime_rationale: Bearish (0.63): $80K resistance firmly rejected, $110M liquidations, hawkish Fed hold, geopolitical risk-off, leadership uncertainty. Prefer SHORT bias or cautious entries. Next binary catalyst: NFP May 8.
